title: A bill authored by Senator Dave Cortese (D-San Jose) to reform California’s “felony murder special circumstance” law is moving forward.
prompt_preview:
[1 chunks] Task: Extract all distinct claims about AI or emerging technology in this press release. Do NOT assume a fixed number.
Output JSON only matching the provided schema.
Return concise, standalone claim_texts that could be tested against scientific literature. Exclude purely procedural statem

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Current California law mandates a sentence of death or life without the possibility of parole for anyone convicted of 'murder with special circumstances.'",
    "Under current law, those involved in certain felonies can face severe punishments even if the death is accidental.",
    "SB 300 will reverse the injustices of current life without parole sentencing law by restoring discretion to judges.",
    "SB 300 will disallow individuals from being sentenced to death or life without parole if they did not directly cause a death, aid a killing, or have intent to kill.",
    "Individuals sentenced under the current law would be eligible for resentencing under SB 300.",
    "On average, 130 people a year in California are sentenced to die in prison for a crime they did not commit.",
    "80% of people serving life without parole in California are people of color, with nearly 70% being Black or Latinx.",
    "The average age of a person at the time of an offense for which a life without parole sentence is imposed due to a special circumstance finding is 19.",
    "Currently, over 5234 people in California are serving life without parole sentences, with 2303 of those being 25 years or younger at the time of the offense.",
    "Taxpayers pay an average of $112,600 per year to incarcerate a young and healthy person serving a life without parole sentence in California."
  ]
}
```
